flashcards.front: What are the two main types of randomized algorithms?
flashcards.back: 1. Las Vegas algorithms (always correct, runtime varies) 2. Monte Carlo algorithms (fixed runtime, may have errors)

flashcards.front: What is the key advantage of Karger's algorithm for min-cut?
flashcards.back: Achieves O(n²) time complexity vs deterministic algorithms' O(mn²) complexity for graph cuts

flashcards.front: What probabilistic method underlies Bloom filters?
flashcards.back: Hash functions with probabilistic false positives but no false negatives

flashcards.front: Why is randomization crucial in primality testing?
flashcards.back: Deterministic tests are computationally expensive, randomized methods (Miller-Rabin) offer practical efficiency

flashcards.front: What is the expected case time complexity of Randomized Quicksort?
flashcards.back: O(n log n) due to probabilistic pivot selection balancing partitions
Expected number of trials to collect all n coupons is Θ(n log n)

flashcards.front: What is the main drawback of Monte Carlo algorithms?
flashcards.back: Possibility of incorrect outputs requiring error probability management

flashcards.front: How does reservoir sampling handle streaming data?
flashcards.back: Maintains uniform probability sample without knowing total size in advance

flashcards.front: What makes Freivald's algorithm for matrix verification efficient?
flashcards.back: Uses randomization to verify AB=C in O(n²) vs O(n³) matrix multiplication

flashcards.front: Explain the power of two choices paradigm
flashcards.back: Randomly selecting best of two options dramatically improves load balancing outcomes

flashcards.front: What is the Schwartz-Zippel lemma used for?
flashcards.back: Probabilistic polynomial identity testing with applications in circuit verification

flashcards.front: How do randomized algorithms handle adversarial inputs?
flashcards.back: Internal randomness makes algorithm behavior input-independent, preventing worst-case exploitation
